Downscaling for Climate Scenarios

Developed by Tragsatec for AEMET

Home Data Methodology Evaluation Downscaling Applications Information

METHODS

For the evaluation, observations from the AEMET network and different reanalysis products (ERA5, ERA5-Land, E-OBS, CERRA, CERRA-Land, and COSMO-REA6) and satellite data (CLARA-A3) were considered, with the aim of finding the reanalysis most similar to the available meteorological observations. For more information, see the Data section.

Each variable has observational time series of varying completeness and consistency. Therefore, a sensitivity analysis was first performed in which, for each variable, the minimum station completeness (always greater than 50%) that, over a given period, yields the largest number of available stations was selected. This analysis was performed for a period after 1995 (the limiting factor for COSMO-REA6).

Variables Minimum Station Completeness Best Possible Period Number of Stations
Temperature 80% 1995-2018 974
Precipitation 80% 1995-2018 1728
Wind Speed 50% 2007-2018 332
Relative Humidity 75% 2009-2018 610
Short-wave Surface Radiation 80% 2000-2020 66

To perform the evaluation, all datasets were compared with AEMET internal observation network, taking into account that wind speed and specific humidity data were not available from CERRA-Land and that only CERRA, CERRA-Land, and CLARA-A3 were used for radiation. This comparison was carried out using Python software pyGridEval, which returns a large number of statistical metrics that allow for comparison between datasets. You can access pyGridEval (Carlos Correa) in the following link.

María Ortega, Irene Rodríguez-Muñoz, Jesús Gutiérrez-Fernández (Tragsatec) ● Esteban Rodríguez-Guisado (AEMET)
